plan.failureSummary: The test failed in DETERMINISTIC mode because it attempted to fill a 'Last Name' field using cached selectors, but the application UI now displays a 'Your Name' field instead.
observedIndicators: Deterministic mode used cached selectors that no longer match the UI structure
Failure screenshot shows 'Your Name' field while tool calls targeted 'Last Name'
Historical pass rate (57%) and alternating success/failure pattern suggest an unstable or dynamic UI environment
The overall objective 'Fill out the all of the survey questions' is semantically flexible and likely to succeed with a fresh AI run

remediationSteps.summary: Clear AI cache and retry test.
remediationSteps.details: Trigger a retry of the test with the 'clearPageAiCache' directive enabled. This will force the Donobu agent to re-analyze the current page state rather than relying on stale cached selectors.

Investigate why the survey form fields ('Last Name' vs 'Your Name') are changing. If this is due to A/B testing or an unstable staging environment, consider stabilizing the test data or environment to reduce cache invalidation.

plan.notes: The failure screenshot clearly shows the form fields have changed compared to what the deterministic cache expected (selectors for 'input-lastname' and 'Last Name *' were used, but only 'Your Name' and 'Contact Email' are visible). This is a classic stale cache scenario where the UI structure evolved. Clearing the cache will allow the autonomous agent to adapt to the new form structure.
